paper_title: Lake-Level Variations During the Early Pleistocene Apsheronian Regional Stage

methods.description: This method involves reconstructing lake-level oscillations based on a detailed sedimentary log along the Hajigabul section, focusing on various sediment characteristics.

steps.description: Field observations focused on variations in lithologies, stacking patterns, colours, sedimentary structures and grain-size distributions, as well as the mineralogy, sorting, rounding, sphericity, texture and maturity of the sediment grains.
steps.searchable_summary: collect field data on sediment characteristics

steps.description: Sediment colours were determined on fresh rocks using Munsell Soil Color Charts.
steps.searchable_summary: determine sediment colors using Munsell Soil Color Charts

steps.description: Petrographic optical microscopic descriptions were performed on 30 lm thick thin-sections, made perpendicular to the progradation direction of the observed sedimentary structures.
Palaeocurrent directions were measured on three-dimensional cross-beds and corrected by unfolding the bedding planes.
Measurements were subsequently statistically distributed over 12 intervals of 30° and plotted in a rose diagram with a maximum representability of 50%.
steps.searchable_summary: plot palaeocurrent directions in a rose diagram

methods.description: This method involves comparing sedimentary facies to established classifications and analyzing faunal assemblages to support environmental reconstructions.

steps.description: Sedimentary facies were compared to well-established classifications (Postma, 1990; Miall, 2006), as well as several detailed referenced studies.
steps.searchable_summary: compare sedimentary facies to established classifications

steps.description: Taphonomic and mollusc observations provided additional information such as depositional energy, water depth and salinity, supporting environmental reconstructions.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze taphonomic and mollusc data for environmental reconstruction

methods.description: This method involves creating a facies depth rank curve and performing cyclostratigraphical analyses to estimate the frequency and timing of environmental changes.

steps.description: A facies depth rank curve was created ranging from 'I' for the deepest deposits, up to 'VII' for the shallowest deposits.
Cyclostratigraphical analyses were subsequently realized on the facies depth rank curve using standard settings.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cyclostratigraphical analyses on depth rank curve

steps.description: Data were normalized to the unit variance and equally-spaced through linear interpolation.
Blackman-Tukey power spectra were generated with a Bartlett window in the Analyseries 2.0.4b program.
Bandpass filters were applied at 90% confidence levels.
Based on this age model, cyclostratigraphical analyses allowed estimation of the frequency and timing of the rhythmic environmental changes.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate frequency and timing of environmental changes
